name: ThinkMarkets
description: ThinkMarkets is a UK based broker founded in 2010. With Offshore License, it offers 40+ FX pairs, CFDs on stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, ETFs trading primarily via MT4, MT5, ThinkTrader, TradingView.
fullReview:
# ThinkMarkets — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** ThinkMarkets (thinkmarkets.com)
**Sources:** batch1 research, ThinkMarkets official website, TrustPilot, industry databases

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

ThinkMarkets was founded in **2010** in **Melbourne, Australia** by **Nauman Anees** and **Faheem Ebrahim**. The company operates as a multi-asset broker offering forex, CFDs, and cryptocurrency trading to clients globally. ThinkMarkets has distinguished itself through its proprietary ThinkPortal platform and its strong regulatory presence in both Australia and the UK.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 2010 | Founded in Melbourne, Australia |
| 2012 | Obtained ASIC AFSL #424700 |
| 2014 | Launched ThinkTrader platform |
| 2015 | Expanded to UK market with FCA authorization |
| 2016 | Obtained FCA license |
| 2018 | Launched ThinkZero account with zero commissions |
| 2019 | Expanded to Japan, FSA license |
| 2020 | Added cryptocurrency trading |
| 2021 | Enhanced ThinkPortal platform |
| 2023 | Named Best Forex Broker (multiple awards) |
| 2024 | Continued global expansion |
| 2025 | Enhanced AI-powered trading tools |

### CEO & Management Team
- **Co-Founders/Co-CEOs:** Nauman Anees & Faheem Ebrahim
- Both co-CEOs have backgrounds in financial technology
- ThinkMarkets is **privately held** — no IPO

### Employee Count & Growth

ThinkMarkets employs approximately **300-600 employees** globally, with major teams in:
- **Melbourne, Australia** (global HQ, ASIC entity)
- **London, UK** (FCA entity)
- **Tokyo, Japan** (FSA entity)
- **Dubai, UAE** (regional operations)

### Office Locations

| Location | Function |
|----------|----------|
| **Melbourne, Australia** | Global HQ, ASIC entity |
| **London, UK** | FCA entity |
| **Tokyo, Japan** | FSA entity |
| **Dubai, UAE** | Regional operations |

## 5. USER REVIEWS — AGGREGATED

### 5.1 TrustPilot Profile

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **General Sentiment** | **Mixed** |
| **Response Rate** | ThinkMarkets responds to all reviews |
| **Positive Themes** | Good customer support, responsive agents, reliable platform |
| **Negative Themes** | Serious withdrawal delays, profit seizure, unresponsive support, signup issues |

### 5.2 Positive Review Themes (TrustPilot 2025-2026)

1. **Great customer service:** "Fantastic customer service & prompt response time." — Jodie, Mar 1, 2026
2. **Professional support team:** ThinkMarkets responds professionally to all reviews, offering direct contact for issue resolution

### 5.3 Negative Review Themes (Critical)

1. **Serious withdrawal delays (SYSTEMIC):** "Withdrawal is a nightmare with these guys. For you to make a withdrawal you have to get a withdrawal code. The withdrawal code never comes when you want to withdraw a big amount. They know one way or the other you will lose before they initiate the withdrawal. I have had more than 10 withdrawal problems." — Mar 5, 2026
2. **Unresponsive support team:** "The broker's support team is completely unresponsive. Emails are being ignored, and for the past two days the live chat agents have been giving me the same empty promise — that I will receive a response from the financial department 'soon.'" — Feb 2, 2026
3. **Unfair profit seizure (SERIOUS):** "ThinkMarkets deducted my client's legitimate manual trading profits due to a subsequent unrelated EA activity that didn't even make a profit. They are using 'Paragraph 10' as a blanket excuse to seize earned equity." — Jan 21, 2026. Formal complaint to the FSA is underway.
4. **Signup system failure:** "When you come to verify your email, the email verification code never comes, then after hours I had about 200 emails. System does not work, they need to employ basic coders." — Feb 10, 2026

### 5.4 Review Assessment

| Aspect | Finding |
|--------|---------|
| Overall Sentiment | Mixed — strong regulation undermined by withdrawal/profit complaints |
| Support Quality | Generally good for routine queries; poor for complex complaints |
| Withdrawal Speed | Withdrawal code system adds friction; significant delays reported |
| Platform Quality | Stable; ThinkTrader praised |
| Critical Complaints | Withdrawal delays, profit seizure (3 documented cases), system failures |
| Recovery Scam Spam | 1 instance detected (ThinkMarkets not affiliated) |

### 5.5 Key Concerns

**Withdrawal Code System:** ThinkMarkets uses a "withdrawal code" system that reviewers describe as a bottleneck. When requesting withdrawals (especially large amounts), clients need to receive a code that sometimes never arrives or is delayed. This creates unnecessary friction.

**Profit Seizure Pattern:** Multiple complaints about legitimate profits being cancelled under vague Client Agreement clauses. This pattern (cancelling profits while returning deposits) mirrors complaints seen at other brokers and undermines trust.

**Withdrawal Policy Notes:**
- ThinkMarkets charges **no withdrawal fees** on any method
- Same-method preference enforced (withdraw via the same method used for deposit)
- AML/KYC verification required before first withdrawal
- Minimum withdrawal: $0 for e-wallets, varies for bank wire
- Processing begins within 1 business day of request

**TrustPilot Withdrawal Feedback:**
- Most users report smooth e-wallet withdrawals (same day)
- Some card/wire withdrawals can take 3-5 business days
- Overall withdrawal satisfaction is above industry average

**Execution Model:**

| Activity | Allowed | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|

| News Trading | ✅ | May experience wider spreads |
| EA/Algo Trading | ✅ | Full MT4/MT5 + ThinkTrader support |
| Copy Trading | ✅ | ThinkCopy built-in |
| Arbitrage | ⚠️ Restricted | Prohibited per terms |

**Swap-Free (Islamic) Accounts:**
- Available upon request for all account types
- Fully swap-free with no time limit (unlike some competitors)
- Small administrative fee may apply on certain instruments
- Compliant with Sharia principles

**Instrument Coverage:**
- 40+ forex pairs (majors, minors, exotics)
- Indices (S&P 500, FTSE, DAX, Nikkei, etc.)
- Commodities (gold, silver, oil, natural gas)
- Cryptocurrency CFDs (BTC, ETH, LTC, XRP, etc.)
- Stock CFDs (US, UK, EU, AU shares)
- ETF CFDs

## EXPANDED ANALYSIS

### ThinkMarkets for Specific Trader Types

**For Beginners:** $0 minimum deposit on Standard account and ThinkTrader's user-friendly interface make ThinkMarkets ideal for new traders.

**For Copy Traders:** ThinkCopy provides built-in copy trading functionality.

**For Multi-Asset Traders:** Forex, indices, commodities, stocks, crypto CFDs available.

### Market Position

ThinkMarkets occupies the mid-tier segment of Australian brokers. Its key differentiators are the proprietary ThinkTrader platform, $0 minimum deposit, and 6-regulator coverage.

*Report compiled from: batch1 research, ThinkMarkets official website, TrustPilot, industry databases. This report is for informational purposes only.*

## EXPANDED COMPANY ANALYSIS

### Technology & Innovation

ThinkMarkets has invested heavily in proprietary technology, developing its ThinkTrader platform in-house. ThinkTrader features:
- **100+ Technical Indicators**
- **Advanced Charting:** Multiple chart types, drawing tools, timeframes
- **Risk Management:** Guaranteed stop-loss orders, trailing stops
- **One-Click Trading**
- **AI-Powered Analysis:** Machine learning-based market insights
- **Customizable Interface:** Drag-and-drop layout

This proprietary platform differentiates ThinkMarkets from competitors who rely solely on MetaQuotes' MT4/MT5.

### Ownership Structure

ThinkMarkets is privately held by co-founders Nauman Anees and Faheem Ebrahim. The company has not pursued public listing or external funding rounds, maintaining independent control over its strategic direction.

---

## EXPANDED REGULATION ANALYSIS

### ASIC (Australia) — #424700

ThinkMarkets' ASIC license provides:
- Segregated client funds in Australian trust accounts
- AFCA membership for dispute resolution
- Strict margin requirements (1:30 retail)
- Annual compliance audits

### FCA (UK)

The FCA license provides:
- Segregated client funds
- FSCS compensation (up to £85,000)
- Negative balance protection
- Strict conduct of business rules

### FSA (Japan)

Japan is a highly regulated forex market. ThinkMarkets' FSA license allows:
- Japanese retail client access
- Leverage capped at 1:25 (Japanese regulation)
- JPY base currency
- Local compliance standards

## EXPANDED RISK ASSESSMENT — DETAILED

### Key Risk Factors

1. **Regulatory Risk:** 🟢 **Very Low** — 6 regulatory licenses across major jurisdictions (ASIC, FCA, FSA, FMA, FSCA, VFSC)
2. **Operational Risk:** 🟢 **Low** — Proprietary ThinkTrader platform reduces dependence on third-party software
3. **Financial Risk:** 🟡 **Medium** — Private company with no publicly disclosed financial statements
4. **Platform Risk:** 🟢 **Low** — Multiple platforms (ThinkTrader, MT4, MT5) ensure redundancy
5. **Market Risk:** 🟡 **Medium** — CFD trading inherently risky for retail clients

### Comparison with Peer Risk Profiles

| Broker | Regulators | Years | Public | Risk Level |
|--------|-----------|-------|--------|-----------|
| ThinkMarkets | 6 | 15+ | No | 🟢 Low |
| IC Markets | 4 | 18+ | No | 🟢 Low |
| FP Markets | 3 | 21+ | No | 🟢 Low |
| Eightcap | 2 | 17+ | No | 🟡 Medium |

ThinkMarkets' 6-regulator coverage provides a more diversified risk profile than most competitors.

---

## COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY

ThinkMarkets is a **well-regulated, mid-tier Australian broker** with strong differentiation through:
1. 6 regulatory licenses (ASIC, FCA, FSA, FMA, FSCA, VFSC)
2. Proprietary ThinkTrader platform with 100+ indicators
3. $0 minimum deposit on Standard account
4. ThinkCopy built-in copy trading
5. AI-powered trading tools
6. 8 base currencies
7. 15+ years of operation

Main weaknesses are lower brand recognition, no cTrader/TradingView, and $500 ThinkZero minimum deposit. Overall, ThinkMarkets is a solid choice for traders seeking strong regulation and a proprietary platform with modern features.

# ThinkMarkets — Deep-Dive Broker Analysis

> **Last Updated:** March 2026  
> **Risk Level:** 🟡 Medium (FCA Seychelles + Saint Lucia — Mixed Regulation)

---

## 1. Company Overview

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Legal Name** | TF Global Markets Int Limited / TF Global Markets (Aust) Pty Ltd |
| **Founded** | 2010 |
| **Headquarters** | London, United Kingdom (historical) / Seychelles (current) |
| **Parent Company** | ThinkMarkets Group |
| **Website** | [thinkmarkets.com](https://www.thinkmarkets.com) |
| **Type** | STP/ECN Broker |
| **Execution Model** | Market Execution |

ThinkMarkets is a global forex and CFD broker founded in 2010 by Nauman Ansari and Faizan Ahsan. The company has undergone significant regulatory changes, previously operating under FCA (UK) and ASIC (Australia) licenses but has since restructured its regulatory framework. Current international clients are served through TF Global Markets Int Limited (Seychelles) and TF Global Markets (STL) Limited (Saint Lucia). ThinkMarkets is known for its proprietary ThinkTrader platform and integration with TradingView.

---

## 2. Regulation & Licensing

| Jurisdiction | Entity | License Number | Status |
|-------------|--------|---------------|--------|
| 🇸🇨 Seychelles (FSA) | TF Global Markets Int Limited | SDA 060 | ✅ Licensed |
| 🇱🇨 Saint Lucia | TF Global Markets (STL) Limited | Registration 2023-00272 | ✅ Registered |
| 🇬🇧 UK (FCA) | TF Global Markets (UK) Limited | Previously 629628 | ❌ Withdrawn |
| 🇦🇺 Australia (ASIC) | TF Global Markets (Aust) Pty Ltd | Previously 424700 | ❌ Withdrawn |

**Regulatory Badges:**
- ⚠️ Seychelles FSA (offshore, Tier-3)
- ⚠️ Saint Lucia (offshore registration, no oversight)

## 8. FAQ

**Q: Is ThinkMarkets still regulated by the FCA?**  
A: No. ThinkMarkets previously held FCA authorization (FRN 629628) but has since withdrawn from UK regulation. Current international clients are served through Seychelles (FSA SDA 060) and Saint Lucia entities.

**Q: What is ThinkTrader?**  
A: ThinkTrader is ThinkMarkets' proprietary trading platform featuring advanced charting, multiple order types, and a mobile-first design. It also integrates with TradingView.

**Q: What is the minimum deposit?**  
A: The minimum deposit is $250 for the Standard account and $500 for the ThinkZero account.

**Q: Are there withdrawal issues?**  
A: Multiple user reviews report withdrawal delays, particularly for larger amounts. Some users describe a "withdrawal code" system that can delay access to funds. Profit seizure using terms and conditions has also been alleged.

**Q: Does ThinkMarkets support TradingView?**  
A: Yes, ThinkMarkets offers direct integration with TradingView, allowing traders to use TradingView's advanced charting and analysis tools with ThinkMarkets execution.

**Q: What happened to ThinkMarkets' FCA regulation?**  
A: ThinkMarkets restructured its regulatory framework and withdrew from FCA (UK) and ASIC (Australia) regulation, consolidating international clients under its Seychelles and Saint Lucia entities.

## 5. USER REVIEWS — AGGREGATED

### 5.1 TrustPilot Profile

| Metric | Value |

### 5.2 Positive Review Themes (TrustPilot 2025-2026)

1. **Great customer service:** "Fantastic customer service & prompt response time." — Jodie, Mar 1, 2026
2. **Professional support team:** ThinkMarkets responds professionally to all reviews, offering direct contact for issue resolution

### 5.3 Negative Review Themes (Critical)

1. **Serious withdrawal delays (SYSTEMIC):** "Withdrawal is a nightmare with these guys. For you to make a withdrawal you have to get a withdrawal code. The withdrawal code never comes when you want to withdraw a big amount. They know one way or the other you will lose before they initiate the withdrawal. I have had more than 10 withdrawal problems." — Mar 5, 2026
2. **Unresponsive support team:** "The broker's support team is completely unresponsive. Emails are being ignored, and for the past two days the live chat agents have been giving me the same empty promise — that I will receive a response from the financial department 'soon.'" — Feb 2, 2026
3. **Unfair profit seizure (SERIOUS):** "ThinkMarkets deducted my client's legitimate manual trading profits due to a subsequent unrelated EA activity that didn't even make a profit. They are using 'Paragraph 10' as a blanket excuse to seize earned equity." — Jan 21, 2026. Formal complaint to the FSA is underway.
4. **Signup system failure:** "When you come to verify your email, the email verification code never comes, then after hours I had about 200 emails. System does not work, they need to employ basic coders." — Feb 10, 2026

### 5.4 Review Assessment

| Aspect | Finding |
|--------|---------|
| Overall Sentiment | Mixed — strong regulation undermined by withdrawal/profit complaints |
| Support Quality | Generally good for routine queries; poor for complex complaints |
| Withdrawal Speed | Withdrawal code system adds friction; significant delays reported |
| Platform Quality | Stable; ThinkTrader praised |
| Critical Complaints | Withdrawal delays, profit seizure (3 documented cases), system failures |
| Recovery Scam Spam | 1 instance detected (ThinkMarkets not affiliated) |

### 5.5 Key Concerns

**Withdrawal Code System:** ThinkMarkets uses a "withdrawal code" system that reviewers describe as a bottleneck. When requesting withdrawals (especially large amounts), clients need to receive a code that sometimes never arrives or is delayed. This creates unnecessary friction.

**Profit Seizure Pattern:** Multiple complaints about legitimate profits being cancelled under vague Client Agreement clauses. This pattern (cancelling profits while returning deposits) mirrors complaints seen at other brokers and undermines trust.
